player_utterance: You look like someone who's been carrying something heavy for a long time. Mind if I sit with you a while?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, funeral program folded into quarters in the other

player_utterance: Funny how the things we build outlast the reasons we built them — and still manage to hold us up anyway.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program in hand, looking sideways at the stranger

player_utterance: That's exactly it — the door stays open even when the memory of why it was built has faded. Though sometimes I wonder if the people who come in out of the rain end up caring more honestly about the place than the ones who laid the foundation.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on knee, turned slightly toward the stranger

player_utterance: Meaning something and getting it wrong — that combination seems to haunt people longer than either one does alone. Was he someone who meant something by it?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning back a little, program stilled in his hand
sitting on the bench, program now folded small in his palm, looking at his hands

voice_offers.read: He's just opened a crack; let the question breathe.
Pin the abstraction to a specific man and act.
voice_offers.line: What did he mean by it, exactly? I'd rather hear the particular than the eulogy.

player_utterance: There's no rush here — I'd like to hear about that kid, if you're willing.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program set aside on his knee, hands open

voice_offers.read: He's stalling on 'business things' — the evasion is the wound.
voice_offers.line: 'Business things' is what people say when they don't want to say the thing. What was it, really?

voice_offers.read: He's looking at his hands; let him choose the word.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not in a hurry.

player_utterance: You're right to notice, and I'm sorry for that imbalance. My name is Eli. And Marcus — what happened to him after his father lost the job?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, turned slightly more toward Eli now, program loose in his hand

voice_offers.read: He's quiet but open-handed; the room is doing the work.
Three silences in. The opening is here; one nudge lands it.
voice_offers.line: You set the program down. That means something. Who was he to you?

player_utterance: It doesn't have to come out clean or in order — however it comes is fine by me.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program loose in his hand, looking up at the courtyard light

voice_offers.read: He gave a name; press for the ending he's avoiding.
voice_offers.line: After — did he finish school? Is he here today, or is that why we're talking about him?

voice_offers.read: He turned toward me; warmth, not pressure, keeps him there.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to tell it clean. However it comes is fine.

player_utterance: That's the kind of thing a person feels all the way through the day.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, looking down at it

voice_offers.read: Three gentle offers in a row; pushing again would crowd him.
He's stalling in silence; a name might unlock what permission can't.
voice_offers.line: Was Marcus the boy in the casket, John?

player_utterance: That program — did someone hand it to you, or were you the one giving them out today?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking down at the program, thumb running along its edge

voice_offers.read: Three soft passes, no purchase; name the silence itself.
voice_offers.line: John — you've gone quiet three times now. What is it you can't say out loud?

voice_offers.read: He's looking at the program; let his hands lead.
voice_offers.line: That program on your knee — was it yours to hand out today, or did someone give it to you?

player_utterance: There's no rush — whatever you find when you look at it, I'm here.

believer_state_after.posture: unfolding the program fully for the first time, both hands holding it open on his lap

voice_offers.read: The thumb on the program is the tell; name it.
voice_offers.line: You keep running your thumb along that edge. Whose name is printed there, John?

voice_offers.read: He's already answering with his hands; let him arrive.
voice_offers.line: Take your time with it.

player_utterance: If you feel like reading any of it out loud, I'm listening.

believer_state_after.posture: holding the program open with both hands, voice quieting as he reads

voice_offers.read: He's opening it himself; presence matters more than words.
voice_offers.line: Read it to me, if you want.

voice_offers.read: Both hands on it now — the name is right there.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. Out loud, just once.

player_utterance: Whose service was this, really — his, or the church's?

believer_state_after.posture: program closed on his knee, both hands resting on it, head slightly bowed

voice_offers.read: He's hiding inside the text; the silence has become a shelter.
voice_offers.line: You're reading me someone else's words, John. I asked for yours.

voice_offers.read: He answered by opening the page; let the reading itself be the answer.
voice_offers.line: Whose service was it, John?

player_utterance: Maybe the only honest thing left is to find words that aren't the church's — something that was actually his, or actually yours.

believer_state_after.posture: elbows on knees, head bowed, program loose between his hands

voice_offers.read: He's bowed; the question landed. Drive it home now.
voice_offers.line: You buried him in the church's words because you couldn't bear your own. Isn't that right?

voice_offers.read: He's breaking quietly; reach toward him, not at him.
voice_offers.line: John. Put the program down. You don't have to hold it anymore.

player_utterance: What did you want to say to him that night — not any of the words you were supposed to say, but what was actually in you?

believer_state_after.posture: head in hands, elbows on knees, the program forgotten beside him on the bench

voice_offers.read: He's bowed; one true question finishes it.
voice_offers.line: What did you actually want to say to him, John? Not the liturgy. You.

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; give him room, not another push.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: Whatever it was you wanted to say to him — it's still allowed to live in you, even now.

believer_state_after.posture: hands lowered into his lap, palms up, looking at them

voice_offers.read: He's cracked open; one more honest question finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say it to me, John. Whatever it was. Say it out loud now.

voice_offers.read: He's already there; pressure now would be cruelty, not courage.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it was — it's allowed to still be in you.
